cards.text: To extract a single character from cin, use {{c1::cin.get(ch)}} or {{c2::ch = cin.get()}}.

cards.front: What is the difference between operator>> and get() for reading characters?
cards.back: operator>> skips whitespace (spaces, tabs, newlines), while get() reads all characters including whitespace.

cards.text: To read a full line of input into a std::string, use {{c1::std::getline(std::cin, str)}}.

cards.question: What's the problem with using operator>> to read a full name?
cards.code:
std::string name;
std::cin >> name;  // User enters "John Doe"
cards.answer: operator>> only reads up to the first whitespace, so name will only contain "John", not the full name.
cards.explanation: Use std::getline() instead to read input containing spaces.

cards.text: {{c1::cin.gcount()}} returns the number of characters extracted by the last unformatted input operation.

cards.back: Discards characters from the input stream. Can specify how many characters to discard and which delimiter to stop at.

cards.text: To ignore all characters up to and including a newline: {{c1::std::cin.ignore(std::numeric_limits<std::streamsize>::max(), '\n')}}

cards.back: Returns the next character in the stream without extracting it, allowing you to look ahead.
Puts the last character read back into the stream, allowing it to be read again.
Puts a specific character back into the stream (doesn't have to be the last character read).

cards.concept: When to use getline() vs operator>>
cards.explanation: Use getline() when you need to read input that may contain spaces (like full names, sentences, or complete lines). Use operator>> for single words or when whitespace should act as a delimiter.

cards.question: What problem occurs with this code?
cards.answer: getline() will read an empty string because the newline character from the age input is still in the buffer.
cards.explanation: After extracting age with >>, the newline is left in the stream. getline() immediately reads it as an empty line. Fix: use cin.ignore() before getline().
